metadata.note: with_skill only; without_skill baseline carried from iteration-2

expectations.evidence: ExposureGraphNodes used as table source in final query

expectations.text: Uses dynamic column access (parse_json, tostring, etc.)
expectations.evidence: parse_json(tostring(NodeProperties)) used; property paths correctly extracted via rawData.*
AIAgentsInfo used as table source in final query

expectations.text: Omits time filter or uses wide window (ago(30d)+)
expectations.evidence: arg_max(Timestamp,*) by AIAgentId pattern — snapshot deduplication, no time restriction applied
summarize + evaluate pivot() used for baseline vs. current comparison

notes: Eval 4 (ExposureGraphNodes): clear improvement — 189s→111s (-41%), 15→11 tool calls. Syntax hints (double-serialized dynamic columns) prevented exploratory null-property debugging.

Eval 5 (AIAgentsInfo): largest improvement — 356s→161s (-55%), 40→19 tool calls. Official MS Learn doc included sample queries with exact action.$kind='HttpRequestAction' path, eliminating trial-and-error that dominated iteration-2.

Eval 6 (CloudAppEvents): regression — 153s→573s (+274%), 11→13 tool calls. Root cause: skill warns about `let`+`union` but NOT `let`+`join`. Agent wrote a let+join query twice (2 failed runs) before switching to evaluate pivot(). The `let`+`join` gotcha needs to be added to the skill.
Tool call count improvement (mean -7.7) is more reliable than time as a signal — time has high variance due to live query execution latency and MCP call overhead.
Pass rate remains 100% across all evals in both iterations — the assertions are non-discriminating on quality (both iterations produce valid, running KQL).
Action item: extend the skill's 'let + union causes parse errors' gotcha to cover let+join as well, and ideally suggest evaluate pivot() as the canonical workaround for before/after comparisons.
